steps.title: Identify the needed control
steps.content: The conclusion is that M. collaris associates T. caesius alarm calls with danger. The strongest support would show that the birds are not merely reacting to any sound or to the researchers' presence, so a control sound matters for isolating the cause.

steps.title: Isolate the alarm call effect
steps.content: Choice A weakens the conclusion by showing a reaction before sounds were played. Choice B concerns other species, and Choice C only shows consistency across different callers. A no-reaction result for random-noise control sounds directly supports the specific alarm-call interpretation. The answer is D.
